title: CMake Project Configuration with Presets
summary: Set up the CMake build system for the Calculator GUI application. This includes the top-level CMakeLists.txt, CMake presets for Debug, Release, and Test configurations, dependency management for the GUI toolkit (Qt6 or Dear ImGui via SDL2), and the foundational project structure. All subsequent tickets depend on this configuration being in place.

acceptance_criteria.description: All requirements with Verification=Automated have passing tests
cmake --preset test && ctest --preset test succeeds end-to-end
Code review approved
Directory structure matches Design Notes specification

title: GUI Window Creation
summary: Create the main application window for the Calculator using a cross-platform GUI toolkit. The window should have a fixed size appropriate for a calculator layout, a title bar reading "Calculator", and a clean application lifecycle (initialize, run event loop, teardown). The architecture must cleanly separate the windowing/rendering layer from the calculator logic to support testability.

acceptance_criteria.description: All requirements with Verification=Automated have passing tests
Manual inspection confirms window renders with correct title
Code review confirms separation of concerns (R5)
Builds cleanly with all three CMake presets

files.description: Updated to instantiate Application and call run()
Add new source files to calculator target

title: Calculator UI Components and Logic
summary: Build the full calculator user interface and computation logic. This includes a display screen showing input and results, a number pad (0-9 with decimal point), arithmetic operation buttons (+, -, x, /), an Enter/equals button to evaluate, and a Clear button to reset. The calculator logic must be fully decoupled from the GUI rendering so that the Calculator class can be tested independently without any GUI dependencies.

acceptance_criteria.description: All requirements with Verification=Automated have passing tests
Code review confirms Calculator class has zero GUI dependencies (R10)
Manual inspection confirms UI layout matches Design Notes grid
Builds cleanly with all three CMake presets

files.description: Wire UI rendering and input handling to Calculator

title: Unit Tests with GTest — 100% Code Coverage
summary: Write comprehensive unit tests for the Calculator class using Google Test (GTest). Tests must achieve 100% line and branch coverage of the Calculator class logic. The test executable is built exclusively under the test CMake preset and run via ctest --preset test. Coverage reports are generated using gcov/lcov and validated as part of the quality gate.

acceptance_criteria.description: All requirements with Verification=Automated have passing tests
ctest --preset test runs all tests successfully
Coverage report shows 100% line coverage on Calculator.cpp and Calculator.h
Code review approved

files.description: GTest test suite for Calculator class
CMake module for coverage report generation (optional, may inline in tests/CMakeLists.txt)
Add CalculatorTest target, link GTest, register with CTest
Add coverage report generation step if needed

title: CI Integration and Coverage Reporting
summary: Create a GitHub Actions CI pipeline that builds the calculator project with all three CMake presets, runs the test suite, generates a coverage report, and enforces the 100% coverage gate on the Calculator class. This provides the traceability loop: ticket -> code -> test -> coverage -> CI verification, ensuring the project can serve as a traceable reference implementation.

acceptance_criteria.description: All requirements with Verification=Automated have passing tests
CI pipeline runs green on a clean push to main
Coverage report artifact is downloadable from CI
Code review approved
